title: ranunculus flabellatus var. cinerascens
intro: Ranunculus flabellatus var. cinerascens, also known as the desert buttercup, is a rare and small perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is characterized by its delicate, white or pale yellow flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The desert buttercup can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and its medicinal properties have been used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
